surface.name: Create a standalone AWS account and complete console orientation
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Create a standalone AWS account and complete console orientation. AWS does not select one first cloud resource, but its official Management Console guide defines a complete beginner route with an explicit conclusion after account access, navigation, search, CloudShell, and dashboard orientation. Prior surface before policy commit: AWS platform, service, and workload not selected.

alternatives_considered.name: AWS Management Console orientation
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This official guide reaches console-navigation familiarity, then recommends separate hands-on service guides rather than a cloud workload outcome.

alternatives_considered.name: Launch a first EC2 instance
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a service-specific compute path. AWS's general getting-started material does not designate EC2 over the other services and onboarding tasks.

entry_point.developer_intent: Start building on AWS without a preselected AWS service, workload, or interface.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived after discovery.
No AWS service, workload, interface, account state, or region has been selected.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The selected standalone-account orientation route demonstrates first success when the developer reaches the guide's Conclusion after locating the console's core controls and widgets.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The developer reaches the documented Congratulations conclusion after locating the console's core controls and widgets.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The developer reaches the documented Congratulations conclusion after locating the console's core controls and widgets.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The developer reaches the documented Congratulations conclusion after locating the console's core controls and widgets.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: Standalone signup requires a root-user email address that can receive the verification code, an AWS account name, a reachable phone number, and contact and billing addresses.
A valid payment method is mandatory before standalone AWS signup can continue.
A service or intended platform outcome must be selected before a resource-creation first mile can be followed.

primary_path.action: Open AWS's general Getting Started Resource Center.
details: The page recommends six onboarding tasks for beginners.
Those tasks teach launching and configuring several services across categories.
primary_path.input: Intent to start building on AWS.
primary_path.output: A set of onboarding tasks, tutorials, and decision guides.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer reaches AWS's documented onboarding choices.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No wait is documented.

primary_path.action: For a new standalone account, open Sign up for AWS, enter the root-user email address and AWS account name, and choose Verify email address.
details: AWS sends a verification code to the root-user email address.
For a business account, AWS recommends a protected corporate distribution address.
primary_path.input: Root-user email address and AWS account name.
primary_path.success_signal: The verification code is sent.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The email address must be reachable; organization-managed member accounts use a separate AWS Organizations path.

primary_path.action: Enter the email verification code, verify it, then create and confirm the root-user password.
details: The password must contain 8 to 128 characters, use at least three documented character classes, and differ from the account name and email address.
primary_path.input: Email code and compliant password.
primary_path.success_signal: AWS advances to account-plan selection.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An invalid or unavailable code or noncompliant password blocks continuation.

primary_path.action: Choose the AWS account plan, enter contact information, read and accept the AWS Customer Agreement.
details: AWS provides separate account-creation instructions for India.
primary_path.input: Plan choice, contact details, and agreement acceptance.
primary_path.output: Account profile ready for billing verification.
primary_path.success_signal: The billing-information page is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The customer agreement must be accepted.

primary_path.action: Enter a valid payment method and billing address.
details: A different billing address can be supplied.
AWS states that signup cannot proceed without a valid payment method.
primary_path.input: Payment and billing information.
primary_path.output: Payment method submitted for account verification.
primary_path.success_signal: AWS permits the signup flow to continue.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid payment information blocks signup; AWS India has separate CVV, possible bank OTP, and refundable 2 INR verification behavior.

primary_path.action: If prompted, confirm identity by receiving an SMS code at the supplied phone number and entering it.
details: The phone must be reachable in the next few minutes.
primary_path.input: Reachable phone number and SMS code.
primary_path.success_signal: AWS accepts the verification code and continues.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: This step is conditional; failure to receive or enter the code prevents completion.

primary_path.action: Wait for the AWS account-activation email, then sign in to the AWS Management Console.
details: AWS says activation usually takes a few minutes but can take up to 24 hours.
Check the spam folder if the message is not visible.
primary_path.input: Completed signup.
primary_path.output: Activated AWS account and console access.
primary_path.success_signal: The activation email arrives and console sign-in succeeds.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Activation can remain pending for up to 24 hours.

primary_path.action: Open Sign up for AWS, enter the root-user email address and AWS account name, and choose Verify email address.
details: AWS sends a verification code to the root-user email address.
primary_path.input: Reachable root-user email and account name.
primary_path.success_signal: AWS sends the email verification code.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An unavailable email address or an organization-managed member-account requirement prevents the standalone path.

primary_path.action: Enter and verify the email code, then create and confirm a compliant root-user password.
details: The password must be 8 to 128 characters, use at least three documented character classes, and differ from the account name and email.
primary_path.input: Email verification code and root-user password.
primary_path.success_signal: AWS advances to account-plan selection.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid code or noncompliant password blocks signup.

primary_path.action: Choose the AWS account plan, provide contact information, and accept the AWS Customer Agreement.
details: AWS documents a separate account-creation route for India.
primary_path.input: Plan choice, contact details, and agreement acceptance.
primary_path.output: Account profile ready for billing verification.
primary_path.success_signal: The billing-information page opens.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Incomplete details or failure to accept the agreement prevents continuation.

primary_path.action: Enter a valid payment method and billing address.
details: AWS states that signup cannot proceed without a valid payment method.
primary_path.input: Payment and billing information.
primary_path.success_signal: AWS permits signup to continue.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid payment information blocks signup; AWS India has separate CVV, bank OTP, and refundable verification behavior.

primary_path.action: If AWS prompts for identity confirmation, receive the SMS code at the supplied phone number and enter it.
details: The phone must be reachable during signup.
primary_path.input: Reachable phone number and SMS code.
primary_path.success_signal: AWS accepts the verification code and continues.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: This step is conditional; failure to receive or enter the code prevents completion when it is requested.

primary_path.action: Choose an AWS Support plan and select Complete sign up.
primary_path.input: Support-plan choice.
primary_path.success_signal: The activation-pending confirmation page appears.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The account remains unusable until activation finishes.

primary_path.action: Wait for the AWS account-activation email.
details: AWS says activation usually takes a few minutes but can take up to 24 hours.
A newly created account might not have access to all services required by the console tutorial for up to 24 hours.
primary_path.input: Completed signup.
primary_path.success_signal: The activation email arrives.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Check spam and allow up to the documented 24-hour window before treating activation as failed.

primary_path.action: Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open Console Home.
details: The console guide recommends the latest Chrome or Firefox.
primary_path.input: Activated AWS account and root-user credentials.
primary_path.success_signal: The console dashboard appears.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Sign-in, browser, or new-account service-access delay prevents the orientation steps.

primary_path.action: Open the account-information menu and review the account ID, current user, and links for account, organization, quotas, billing, security credentials, and settings.
primary_path.input: Console Home navigation bar.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer can identify the account and its management links.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing navigation or access restrictions prevent completion of this guide section.

primary_path.action: Open the Region selector and switch the console to another available AWS Region.
details: The selector displays the region group, region name, and region string; global services show Global.
primary_path.input: Console session and Region choice.
primary_path.output: Console scoped to the selected Region.
primary_path.success_signal: The navigation bar shows the newly selected Region.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A service can be global or unavailable in the selected Region.

primary_path.action: Use the service selector and search box to locate services, features, documentation, tutorials, and other AWS results.
details: Services can also be marked as favorites and pinned to the navigation bar.
primary_path.input: Console navigation and a search term.
primary_path.output: Navigable service categories and search results.
primary_path.success_signal: The selector and search return the documented categories of results.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An unmatched query omits result sections with no matches.

primary_path.action: Select the AWS CloudShell icon and launch the browser-based shell in the current Region.
details: CloudShell is pre-authenticated with the console credentials and its files are Region-specific.
primary_path.input: Authenticated console session and selected Region.
primary_path.success_signal: The CloudShell prompt opens and shows the current Region.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Account age, service availability, permissions, or shell launch failure can prevent CloudShell access.

primary_path.action: Return to Console Home, review the default dashboard widgets, and reach the guide's Conclusion.
details: The guide covers static getting-started widgets plus AWS Health, cost and usage, favorites, recently visited, and Trusted Advisor.
The conclusion explicitly congratulates the developer for finishing the Management Console guide.
primary_path.input: Completed account, Region, navigation, search, and CloudShell orientation.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer reaches the documented Congratulations conclusion after locating the console's core controls and widgets.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: This terminal proves console familiarity, not creation of a cloud resource; a service-specific tutorial is required for that different outcome.

branches.condition: The account is created inside an AWS Organization.
branches.path: An authorized principal in the organization's management account uses the AWS Organizations CreateAccount operation; a standalone account cannot be created through the AWS CLI or API.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The organization process replaces standalone signup, payment, phone, and support-plan pages and introduces organization governance.

branches.condition: The account's contact and billing address is in India.
branches.path: Follow the AWS India account-creation guide, including CVV, any bank one-time password, and the refundable 2 INR payment-method verification charge.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The account contract and payment verification differ from the outside-India path.

branches.condition: Console orientation is selected as the intended first success.
branches.path: Sign in, review account information, switch Regions, use the service selector and search, launch CloudShell, and review dashboard widgets until the guide's congratulations conclusion.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Success is familiarity with console navigation, not creation of a cloud resource.

branches.condition: A running compute resource is selected.
branches.path: Follow the first EC2 instance tutorial, including administrator setup, console familiarity, instance configuration, launch, connection or status verification, and cleanup.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Success becomes a service-specific EC2 instance outcome with possible usage charges.

friction_gates.description: Standalone signup requires root email verification, a root password, plan and contact setup, and agreement acceptance.
AWS will not allow standalone signup to continue until a valid payment method is added.
AWS can require phone ownership verification by SMS during signup.
Account activation usually takes a few minutes and can take up to 24 hours.
AWS requires a choice among multiple services, workloads, and learning outcomes before one resource-oriented first mile exists.
The EC2 candidate requires an administrator user signed into the console.

sources.title: Getting started with AWS
evidence_supported: Need to choose services by use case

sources.title: Getting Started with the AWS Management Console
evidence_supported: Congratulations conclusion and later service tutorials

sources.title: Tutorial 1: Launch my very first Amazon EC2 instance
evidence_supported: Free Tier eligibility and potential charges

sources.title: Getting started with an AWS account
evidence_supported: AWS India and AWS Organizations branches

uncertainties.question: Which AWS service or learning outcome should represent AWS in the cross-platform dataset?
uncertainties.impact: The prerequisite set, steps, cost gates, and terminal signal vary materially.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: AWS's general entry point recommends several onboarding tasks and service-specific tutorials without one normalized first resource outcome.

excluded_after_success: No after-success scope is selected because the platform-wide terminal boundary is unresolved.
Production architecture, security hardening, scaling, observability, and service-specific advanced work are excluded.
